title: Guest Greeting and Complaint Handling
description: How to properly greet a restaurant guest and defuse dissatisfaction before it becomes a bad review.

steps.title: The first 30 seconds rule
steps.content: A guest must be noticed within 10 seconds of entry and seated within 30 seconds. Even if no tables are free — approach immediately, greet them, say: 'A table will open in ~5 minutes, can I offer you the bar?'. Silence is the #1 reason guests walk out.
steps.key_takeaway: 10 seconds for eye contact, 30 seconds for first word. Silence equals rejection.

steps.title: Reading the guest's mood
steps.content: Couple on a date — don't over-talk. Family with kids — immediately offer kids' menu and coloring. Business lunch — minimum words, maximum speed. Tourist — suggest what to order. Adapt to the guest type, don't recite a memorized script.
steps.key_takeaway: Scripts are for rookies. A pro reads the guest in 5 seconds.

steps.title: The LAST method for complaints
steps.content: L — Listen (don't interrupt, let them vent). A — Apologize (sincerely, without excuses: 'I'm so sorry this happened'). S — Solve (offer a fix: replace, comp the item, send a treat). T — Thank (thank them for the feedback). LAST works in 90% of cases.
steps.key_takeaway: Listen → Apologize → Solve → Thank. Never start with 'but…'.

steps.title: What NOT to say
steps.content: Banned phrases: 'Not my section', 'Chef is busy', 'Everyone cooks it this way', 'You're wrong', 'That's just our policy'. Each one triggers a bad review. Replace with: 'Let me help you', 'We'll fix this right now', 'I understand'.
steps.key_takeaway: Five banned phrases. Learn them and never say them.

steps.title: Escalating to the manager
steps.content: Call the manager if: guest demands it, guest raises voice, refund is involved, conflict with another guest, guest is intoxicated. Don't try to solve alone. Your role — keep a friendly tone until a senior arrives. Manager must come within 2 minutes.
steps.key_takeaway: Five situations where you escalate immediately. No heroics needed.

steps.title: Compensation and its limits
steps.content: You can offer yourself: comp a dish, send a compliment from the house (dessert, drink). Manager only: discount on the full bill, refund, free future visit. Never promise what you can't deliver.
steps.key_takeaway: You = compliment. Manager = discount and refund. Clear boundaries.

steps.title: Closing the incident and saying goodbye
steps.content: After resolving — check back in 5 minutes: 'All good?'. Show you remember the situation. On farewell — sincerely apologize again and thank them: 'Thanks for giving us a chance to fix it'. Invite them back. A guest whose complaint was handled well is more loyal than one who never complained.
steps.key_takeaway: A well-handled complaint = +1 regular. A bad one = -10 through word of mouth.

quiz.question: Within how many seconds must a guest be noticed?
quiz.explanation: 10 seconds for eye contact is the international standard.

quiz.question: What does LAST stand for?
quiz.explanation: The classic complaint-handling method: listen, apologize, solve, thank.

quiz.question: Which phrase is banned when speaking to a guest?
quiz.explanation: 'Not my section' triggers bad reviews. The guest doesn't care whose section it is.

quiz.question: When should you call the manager immediately?
options: Guest demands manager or refund is involved
quiz.explanation: Five escalation triggers exist; manager demand and refund are the main ones.

quiz.question: What CAN you offer yourself as compensation?
quiz.explanation: A compliment (dessert, drink) is within your authority. The rest goes through a manager.

manager_checklist: Guest noticed within 10 seconds of entry
Uses LAST on complaints, doesn't make excuses
Escalates to manager in the right situations
Checks back in 5 minutes to verify the fix

kpi.metric: Share of complaints resolved without escalation
kpi.how_to_measure: Incident log: total complaints vs escalations

kpi.metric: Guest return rate after a complaint
kpi.how_to_measure: CRM: track if the guest returned within 60 days
